title: Incorrect result with DISTINCT over distributed and optimize_skip_unused_shards
description:
### Company or project name
CH
### Describe what's wrong
Reference: https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/88281
```
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS data_local;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS data_distributed;
CREATE TABLE data_local ENGINE=Memory() AS SELECT * FROM numbers(2);
CREATE TABLE data_distributed AS data_local ENGINE=Distributed(test_cluster_two_shards, currentDatabase(), data_local, number);
set enable_analyzer=1;
SELECT count() FROM (SELECT DISTINCT number FROM data_distributed) SETTINGS optimize_skip_unused_shards=0;
SELECT count() FROM (SELECT DISTINCT number FROM data_distributed) SETTINGS optimize_skip_unused_shards=1;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS data_local;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS data_distributed;
```
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
Fails both with the old/deprecated analyzer and the current one.
### Expected behavior
Both queries with and without `optimize_skip_unused_shards` should return the same, but they don't. You get:
```
2
4
```
Expected:
```
2
2
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
.
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE data_local AS SELECT number AS id, toString(number) AS value FROM range(10);
CREATE TABLE data_distributed AS SELECT * FROM data_local;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT value FROM data_distributed);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT value FROM data_distributed);
key_bug_features: DISTINCT with optimize_skip_unused_shards in distributed queries
